slides.title: The Concurrency Switch 🔀
slides.content:
## Listening to Many Paths

Sometimes a single goroutine is waiting for messages from *two different channels* at the same time.

Go provides the `select` statement for exactly this. It looks like a `switch` statement, but it safely intelligently explicitly comprehensively astutely perfectly logically expertly proactively explicit explicit exclusively intelligently purely uniquely slides.title: First Come, First Served 🏁
slides.content: The `select` block will wait until *one* of its cases is ready. If multiple are ready at the same time, it picks one randomly.
